title: Developmental Origins of Health and Disease
authors: Enciso JM, Devaskar SU / Fanaroff & Martin 2025

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Developmental Origins of Health and Disease?
DOHaD คือกรอบทฤษฎีที่บอกว่า สิ่งที่เกิดใน NICU วันนี้ส่งผลต่อสุขภาพของผู้ใหญ่ทศวรรษข้างหน้า. Barker hypothesis: LBW → ↑ CVD/HTN/T2DM. สิ่งที่ต้องรู้: (1) Catch-up growth = short-term gain, long-term pain — ↑ visceral adiposity + metabolic syndrome; (2) mismatch ระหว่าง in utero deprivation + postnatal excess คือ mechanism หลัก; (3) Epigenetics คือ link ระหว่าง environment → genome → phenotype — inheritable ข้ามรุ่น; (4) Maternal obesity, diabetes, microbiome, toxins, infections — ทั้งหมดส่งผ่าน placenta ถึงลูก; (5) NICU nutrition walks a fine line — adequate brain growth vs avoiding excess that programs adult disease. เราคือ physician คนแรกที่ set life trajectory ของเด็กเหล่านี้.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย Barker hypothesis และ thrifty phenotype: กลไกที่เชื่อม LBW → adult CVD/T2DM
วิเคราะห์ evidence จาก Dutch Hunger Winter 1944–45: ผลต่อลูกในระยะสั้น/ยาว ตาม trimester ที่ exposed
อธิบาย catch-up growth trade-off: ทำไม rapid catch-up → ↑ metabolic syndrome; breast milk vs formula ใน SGA
อธิบาย mismatch concept: developmental plasticity + epigenetic tuning → phenotype match; ผลต่อ LGA และ IUGR
ระบุกลไก vascular programming: arterial stiffness, endothelial dysfunction, microvascular rarefaction, VEGF/NO axis
อธิบาย gestational diabetes → offspring metabolic risk: Pima Indian sibship study, intrauterine hyperglycemia
ระบุบทบาท maternal microbiome: mode of delivery, breastfeeding, maternal obesity → dysbiosis → disease risk
อธิบาย DOHaD neuropsychiatric: maternal overnutrition, infection, stress → cytokines, serotonin, BDNF → ASD/ADHD/depression
ระบุ developmental origins of cancer: prenatal genetic hits ใน leukemia + solid tumors
อธิบาย epigenetic mechanisms: DNA methylation, histone modification, microRNAs — RXRA, PPARG-C1A, IGF2 examples
ประยุกต์ DOHaD ใน NICU practice: nutrition decisions, toxin exposure, intervention caution
1. Barker Hypothesis — Fetal Origins of Adult Disease

Barker Hypothesis: LBW → Adult CVD/HTN/T2DM
Barker DJ Ann Hum Biol 2009: ทฤษฎีเริ่มต้นจาก epidemiologic association ระหว่าง small size at birth → risk ของ coronary artery disease, hypertension, stroke, T2DM, osteoporosis ใน adulthood. Replicated ใน populations หลายชาติพันธุ์. Dutch famine 1944–45: สตรีที่เคยได้รับอาหารปกติแล้วถูก restrict ระหว่างตั้งครรภ์ — ลูกที่โตเป็นผู้ใหญ่อายุ 50–60 ปี มี ↑ HTN, CVD mortality/morbidity, glucose intolerance/insulin resistance. ต่อมาขยายเป็น 'developmental origins of health and disease (DOHaD)' — ไม่ใช่แค่ caloric restriction; triggers ทั้ง nutrient deficiency/excess, stress, inflammation, toxins, drugs → fetal hormonal-metabolic milieu → adult chronic disease.

Thrifty phenotype: fetal adaptations ใน adverse environment = energy conservation at the expense of growth; after birth masked แต่ tipping point ไปสู่ disease state เมื่อ nutritional/metabolic stressors เพิ่มขึ้น
การ express ไม่ได้จำกัดแค่ aging adults — ปัจจุบัน lifestyle ↑ caloric intake + inactivity → features เห็นตั้งแต่ childhood/teenage years
สำคัญ: fetus ที่ถูก nutritional perturbation โดยไม่มีผลต่อ growth (size ปกติ) ก็ยังพัฒนา adult chronic disease — birth weight เป็นแค่ surrogate, ไม่ใช่ full picture

Chronic diseases attributed to DOHaD (Box 15.1 Fanaroff 2025): T2DM, obesity, dyslipidemia, HTN, CAD, stroke, CKD, liver disease, COPD/asthma, immune dysfunction, PCOS, premature pubarche, osteoporosis, Alzheimer, depression/anxiety/ADHD/autism/schizophrenia, cancer, shortened life span
1.1 Dutch Hunger Winter 1944–45 — Key Epidemiology

rows: ↑ Glucose intolerance/IR, HTN; protective if adequate postnatal nutrition (Leningrad siege)
NO adult chronic disease — postnatal nutrition match = protective

blocks.text: Key insight: mismatch ระหว่าง in utero deprivation + postnatal excess คือ mechanism หลัก — ไม่ใช่แค่ deprivation ล้วนๆ.
2. Postnatal Growth, Catch-Up Growth, and Nutrition

Catch-Up Growth Trade-off: Short-Term Gain, Long-Term Pain
LBW + slow growth pattern → exponential catch-up growth during childhood → ↑ risk metabolic syndrome (obesity, IR, HTN, dyslipidemia) (Reinehr T Eur J Endocrinol 2009; Beukers F Arch Dis Child 2013). Mechanism ของ catch-up growth: favors nutrient deposition in white adipose tissue (particularly visceral) + rearrangement of skeletal muscle mitochondria + ↑ oxidative injury → shortened telomere length (Davy P Placenta 2009:30:539). ผล: set stage สำหรับ metabolic syndrome, T2DM, CAD ใน adulthood. Rapid postnatal catch-up growth = 'short-term gain, long-term pain'. แต่ absence of catch-up growth → glucose tolerance, lean body, ↓ CAD with longevity — BUT may negatively affect cognition and reproductive capacity. ดังนั้น: moderate long-term growth > rapid catch-up ใน short period.
2.1 Breast Milk vs Formula ใน SGA Infants
de Zegher F et al J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2013;98:1242: SGA infants + maternal breast milk → ↓ adiposity + normal adiponectin vs standard/protein-enriched formula → ↑ fat mass + ↓ adiponectin
Singhal A et al Am J Clin Nutr 2010;92:1133: fortified formula associated with ↑ BP + ↑ insulin resistance in childhood — 2 RCTs
Practical: breast milk preferred for SGA infants — ↓ metabolic programming risk; ไม่ rush protein-enriched formula เพื่อ catch-up growth
2.2 LGA Infants — Same Trajectory, Different Direction
LGA (maternal DM หรือ obesity): deprivation occurs in 1st trimester → rapid catch-up growth 3rd trimester → LGA; further postnatal escalated growth → earlier adult chronic diseases
Weight status first 6 months of life predicts obesity at age 3 + future mortality (Taveras EM Pediatrics 2009;123:1177)
Visceral adiposity > subcutaneous fat → more dangerous; lipotoxicity: fat accumulation in liver, pancreatic beta cells, skeletal muscle, bone marrow → metabolic disruption
Biomarkers of risk: circulating insulin (C-peptide), leptin, adiponectin, cytokine concentrations — preempt childhood/adolescent insulin resistance disorders
3. Mismatch Concept and Developmental Plasticity

Mismatch Concept — กุญแจสำคัญของ DOHaD
Mismatch: ยิ่ง disparity ระหว่าง developmental environment (prenatal/early postnatal) กับ later environment มากเท่าไหร่ → ยิ่ง ↑ risk of chronic disease. Pathway 1 (deprived early): compromised maternal nutrition, toxins, infections, stress, hypoxia. Pathway 2 (excess later): energy-dense foods + sedentary lifestyle → amplifies mismatch. Key: epigenetic processes during developmental plasticity tune phenotype to predicted environment — ถ้า environment หลัง birth ไม่ match prediction → disease. นี่มีความสำคัญใน developing societies ที่เปลี่ยน socioeconomic status อย่างรวดเร็ว + immigrant families จากประเทศยากจนมาอยู่ Western countries.
VLBW infants: deprivation = early postnatal life → catch-up post-neonatal — earlier the deprivation phase followed by catch-up growth, the more significant the adult consequences
NICU implication: NICU drugs, nutrition, interventions ระหว่าง period of developmental plasticity → potential to alter life course — caution before introducing untested interventions (Fanaroff 2025)
Stressors ที่เกิดใน NICU (toxins, hypoxia, nutrition, infections, inflammation) → effects similar to in utero stressors → long-term DOHaD consequences
4. Fetal Vascular Programming — Cardiometabolic Disease
4.1 Large Vessel Structural Changes
LBW/SGA → arterial stiffness + ↑ coronary artery intima-media thickness (Norman M Semin Perinatol 2010)
Excessive gestational weight gain → offspring obesity + ↑ BMI + ↑ systolic BP (Mamun AA Circulation 2009;119:1720)
Animal studies: maternal undernutrition + high-fat diet → ↑ arterial stiffness + ↑ arterial wall thickness → atherosclerosis in offspring (Fan L Int J Obes 2013)
Skilton MR et al Arterioscler Thromb Vasc Biol 2011: fetal growth + preterm birth → ↑ cardiovascular risk factors + arterial health impairment in young adults
4.2 Endothelial Dysfunction

Endothelial Dysfunction ใน LBW/SGA — กลไกและ Vasomotor Impairment
Vasoactive mediators: vasoconstrictors: thromboxane A₂, endothelin-1 (ET-1), PGH₂; vasodilators: NO, PGI₂ (prostacyclin), EDHF. LBW/SGA: impaired vasodilation (↓ flow-mediated dilatation) + evidence of early atherosclerosis (Skilton 2011). Mechanism: ↑ oxidative stress + ↑ ROS → ↓ NO bioavailability. Maternal overnutrition → ↑ placental ROS → neonatal insulin resistance + obesity. SGA + LGA both: ↑ oxidative stress, ↓ antioxidant enzymes, endothelial dysfunction (Jayet PY Circulation 2010;122:488).
4.3 Microvascular Rarefaction
Rarefaction: ↓ vascular density → restricted tissue perfusion + ↑ capillary diffusion distances → ↑ insulin resistance; observed in obesity + HTN
LBW → rarefaction + impaired endothelium-dependent vasodilation of skin microvasculature
SGA babies: ↓ retinal vascularization (↓ VEGF + ↓ IGF-1 in preterm/IUGR) — mechanism overlap กับ ROP pathophysiology (Hellström A Pediatrics 2003;112:1016)
Maternal low-protein diet → ↓ organ + vascular development → ↑ BP + CKD in offspring (Lloyd LJ J Physiol 2012;590:377)
Reduction in VEGF + VEGF receptor expression = mechanism inhibiting microvascular development → sets stage for hypertension, cardiorenal disease
5. Placental Size, Shape, and Adult Disease
Helsinki Birth Cohort (13,345 persons born 1943–44) — Barker DJ et al Int J Dev Biol 2010;54:525: low placental weight + ↓ surface area → ↑ HTN in adulthood
Eriksson JG et al Eur Heart J 2011;32:2297: small placental surface area + placental inefficiency → powerful predictor of ↑ CHD in men
Conversely: large placenta with ↑ surface area + ↑ lobes → ↑ BP (ALSPAC study Bristol UK: higher BW children → ↑ BP age 9 yrs)
Both large AND small placentas can limit nutrient flow → long-term cardiovascular effects — placental efficiency matters เท่ากับ size
Dutch famine cohort: ↓ placental weight + ↑ placental:BW ratio with periconceptual famine exposure (Roseboom TJ Placenta 2011;32:395)
Maternal cotyledons at birth predict BP in childhood (Barker D Placenta 2013;34:672) — ส่ง placenta pathology ทุกราย FGR/SGA
6. Gestational Diabetes and Offspring Metabolic Programming

Intrauterine Hyperglycemia → T2DM + Obesity ใน Offspring
Pima Native American data — Dabelea D et al Diabetes 2000;49:2208: sibship study (same genetic background) — ลูกที่เกิด AFTER แม่พัฒนา T2DM มี ↑ risk T2DM + ↑ BMI กว่าลูกที่เกิด BEFORE — เป็น evidence แก้ confounding ของ genetics. ยืนยัน: intrauterine exposure to hyperglycemia (ไม่ใช่แค่ genetics) → offspring obesity + T2DM. GDM mechanism: maternal diabetes → altered vasculogenesis/angiogenesis/barrier integrity/vascular maturation in fetus → vascular dysfunction → ↑ CVD/metabolic disease in offspring (Leach L Microcirculation 2011).
Epigenetic link — MEST gene (El Hajj N Diabetes 2013;62:1320): hypomethylation of MEST in fetal cord blood + placenta from offspring of GDM mothers → ↑ obesity/metabolic disease risk

Bariatric surgery epigenetics (Guénard F PNAS 2013;110:11439): DNA methylation differences in glucoregulatory, immunologic, cardiovascular genes ใน offspring born before vs after maternal bariatric surgery — direct evidence of nutritional epigenetic programming
Maternal obesity: ↑ BMI → ↑ offspring obesity + diabetes; maternal obesity → ↑ ROS in placenta → neonatal insulin resistance
7. Maternal Microbiome and Early Life Programming
7.1 Microbiome Establishment
Microbial colonization: begins at birth, established in first 3 years, remains stable into adulthood (Sarkar A J Clin Med 2021;10:459)
Maternal microbiome → transferred to infant at birth → important for immune development, inflammatory responses, GI integrity, metabolism, neurodevelopment
Vaginal delivery: transfers maternal vaginal + GI bacteria to infant. C-section: NO maternal bacterial transfer — altered infant microbiome
Breastfeeding: promotes microbiome stability + immune protection from GI infections + allergies vs formula feeding
7.2 Maternal Obesity and Microbiome Dysbiosis
Obese mothers: ↑ Bacteroides, Clostridium, Staphylococcus; ↓ Bifidobacterium → offspring risk ↑ obesity + diabetes
Maternal diet, antibiotic use during pregnancy → alter maternal + infant microbiome composition → affect infant immune development
Altered microbiome → ↑ susceptibility to: obesity, T2DM, CVD, allergic diseases (asthma, atopic dermatitis), IBD, neuropsychiatric disorders (gut-brain axis)
Vaginal microbiome: Lactobacillus dominance (low diversity) → ↓ risk preterm birth + PPROM (Jasarević E Front Neuroendocrinol 2019;55:100797)
8. Developmental Origins of Neuropsychiatric Disorders

Maternal Overnutrition, Stress, Infection → Offspring Neuropsychiatric Disease
Al-Haddad BJS et al JAMA Psychiatr 2019;76:594: large Swedish database study (followed birth → age 41): fetal exposure to maternal infection → ↑ risk autism OR depression. Mechanisms: maternal overnutrition → ↑ proinflammatory cytokines (via TLR4 upregulation) → impaired neurogenesis, neuronal migration, axon guidance, synapse formation. HPA axis: maternal stress/overnutrition → altered cortisol signaling → offspring HPA dysregulation. Serotonin (5-HT): synthesized from maternal l-tryptophan in placenta → maternal overnutrition → impaired brain serotonin signaling → ASD, ADHD, anxiety, mood disorders. BDNF: perinatal stress + inflammation + overnutrition → ↓ BDNF → cognitive deficits.

Neuropsychiatric outcomes attributed to DOHaD: cognitive impairment, anxiety, depression, schizophrenia, ASD, ADHD — via overlapping pathways: inflammatory cytokines, neurotrophic factors, metabolic signaling, microbiome, epigenetics
Maternal overnutrition → insulin + leptin signaling disruption → ↑ metabolic changes affecting neurodevelopment + cognitive function
Emerging: roles of microbiome + epigenetic changes in mediating maternal factors' effects on developing fetal brain
9. Developmental Origins of Cancer
Multi-hit model: prenatal events can provide initial genetic 'hit' — fetal origins of childhood leukemias + solid tumors (Filbin M & Monje M Nat Med 2019;25:367)
Leukemias: prenatal cell of fetal origin → specific gene mutations — ETV6-RUNX1 or MLL-AF4 (ALL); GATA1 (TMD, AMKL in Down syndrome); AML1-ETO + MLL (AML)
Solid tumors: malignant rhabdoid tumors + gliomas — prenatal cell-specific mutations during time-limited windows in embryonic development
High-grade glioma: children vs adolescents/young adults → different mutations in same histone H3 gene; childhood = oligodendroglial precursors; adult = neural stem cells/oligodendrocyte precursors

Birth weight → speculated association with breast, ovarian, colon, lung cancers + blood cancers (Roman E Eur J Cancer 2013;49:1437) — genetics + environment alone cannot fully explain cancer pathogenesis
10. Artificial Reproductive Technology and Embryo Programming
ART ใช้ใน 1–3% ของ live births worldwide (Davies MJ N Engl J Med 2012;366:1803): culture media + techniques → alter embryo environment → altered offspring phenotype
ART → ↑ prematurity, growth restriction, congenital malformations, imprinting disorders (even after correcting for infertility + maternal/paternal age)

Imprinting: differential gene expression by parental origin — paternal genes ↑ placental/fetal growth; maternal genes ↓ placental/fetal size. ART → ↑ imprinting disorders: Beckwith-Wiedemann, Angelman, Prader-Willi
Angelman syndrome: 1/400,000 → 1/20,000 ด้วย ART (↑ 20-fold)
IVF children: ↓ early infant growth → 'catch-up' after 6 months → ↑ BP + altered body composition — same DOHaD trajectory
11. Environmental Toxins, Endocrine Disruptors, and Air Pollution
11.1 Endocrine Disruptors

Endocrine Disruptors — Epigenetic Transgenerational Effects
BPA (bisphenol A) + phthalates (plastics): antiandrogenic compounds → affect endocrine function → ↑ pubertal abnormalities, ovarian/testis disease, obesity — transgenerational epigenetic inheritance: ใน pregnant animal exposed → fetus (2nd gen) + gametes (3rd gen) face same consequences (Manikkam M PLoS One 2013;8:e55387). DES (diethylstilbestrol) — classic example: low-dose DES → offspring LBW + catch-up growth + adult obesity + ↓ activity + abnormal glucose metabolism. BPA → obesity + insulin resistance (Newbold RR Am J Clin Nutr 2011). → Led to push to eliminate plasticizers from infant feeding bottles.
Pesticides + estrogens + antiandrogenic compounds: affect endocrine function; changes epigenetic in nature + inherited; effect may be sex-specific depending on timing of exposure
11.2 Air Pollution
Outdoor sources: vehicle emissions, industrial, residential heating, shipping, agriculture, fires; indoor: cigarette smoke, gas cooking, cleaning products
PM2.5 + ultrafine particles (<0.1 μm) most harmful; pollutants cross placenta → amniotic fluid
Mechanisms: altered immune/inflammatory responses, oxidant injury, epigenetic changes → IUGR, LBW, preterm birth, hypertensive disorders of pregnancy
Nicotine: airway inflammation + thickened airways + abnormal airway branching + ↓ alveolarization + ↓ lung function → ↑ asthma risk in childhood (Kim D Chronic Dis Transl Med 2018;4:75)
Air pollution → ↓ fetal lung development + ↑ allergic disease + asthma in childhood + COPD in adulthood (Brumberg HL Pediatrics 2021;147)
12. Epigenetics — Mechanisms of Fetal Programming
12.1 Three Main Epigenetic Mechanisms

rows: CpG island methylation at gene promoters → heterochromatin → ↓ transcription; relies on one-carbon metabolism (folate, B12, methionine, betaine)
IGF2 hypomethylation ใน Dutch famine periconceptual → ↑ obesity (Vaiserman 2021); MEST hypomethylation ใน GDM offspring
Histone acetylation → euchromatin → ↑ transcription; deacetylation + methylation → condensed heterochromatin → ↓ expression
Histone H3 mutation ใน childhood high-grade glioma — developmental window specific
Short noncoding RNA → bind 3'-UTR of target gene → suppress translation; can regulate DNA methyltransferases indirectly
miR-15b + miR-16 downregulated ใน T2DM discordant twins → target INSR, IRS1, PIK3R1 (Bork-Jensen Diabetologia 2015)

blocks.text: 12.2 One-Carbon Metabolism and DNA Methylation
Methyl donors: folate, methionine, betaine, choline → SAM (S-adenosyl-methionine) → methyl groups to CpG islands via DNA methyltransferases (Dnmts)
Methylated CpG → attracts MeCP2 → recruits histone deacetylases + methylases → condensed heterochromatin → gene repression
Hypomethylation → histone acetylases + demethylases → euchromatin → transcription factor binding → gene activation
Monozygotic twins: subtle DNA methylation differences accumulate over fetal development + extrauterine life → explain phenotypic differences with age (Devaskar SU Rev Endocr Metab Disord 2007)
12.3 Key Epigenetic Biomarkers

RXRA (retinoid X receptor alpha) — Godfrey KM et al Diabetes 2011;60:1528: umbilical cord tissue RXRA promoter methylation at birth → ↑ adiposity in later childhood; stronger predictor than BW or maternal body composition
PPARG-C1A — Clarke-Harris R et al Diabetes 2014;63:2528: promoter methylation in blood at age 5–7 years → stable from childhood to puberty → predicts ↑ adiposity at age 14 (EarlyBird 50 study)
IGF2: hypomethylation of imprinted IGF2 locus in Dutch famine periconceptual exposed survivors → ↑ obesity 6 decades later (Vaiserman A Environ Epigenet 2021;7:dvab013)
Gene networks work in cohesion toward ultimate phenotype — not single-gene effects but panoply of genes working together
12.4 Transgenerational Inheritance
Epigenetic changes are heritable: transmitted from parental → daughter cells through somatic cell division → inherited across multiple generations
Endocrine disruptors: pregnant animal exposed → 2nd generation (fetus) + 3rd generation (gametes of fetus) face same exposure consequences — epigenetic transgenerational inheritance
Aberrant environment → permanent adaptive epigenetic changes → change gene expression → change cell cycle/phenotype → carried transgenerationally = DOHaD mechanism
13. Translation to Neonatology — NICU Practice

NICU: Period of Developmental Plasticity — ทุก Intervention มีผลระยะยาว
Key principle (Fanaroff 2025): NICU nutrition, drugs, interventions ระหว่าง period of developmental plasticity → potential to alter the entire life course. Caution before introducing untested interventions. เด็ก VLBW: deprivation ใน early postnatal life → catch-up growth post-neonatal. Earlier the deprivation + catch-up sequence, the more significant adult consequences. Nutrition walks a fine line: avoid nutritional excess (→ adult disease) vs ensure adequate energy for developing brain (→ neurocognitive outcomes). Breast milk preferred. Moderate sustained growth > rapid catch-up.
13.1 Practical DOHaD Checklist ใน NICU
Nutrition strategy: prefer human milk for SGA/VLBW; avoid protein-enriched formula for rapid catch-up; moderate long-term growth > rapid catch-up
Toxin awareness: BPA-free bottles/tubing; minimize unnecessary drug exposures (sedatives, steroids, antibiotics) during plasticity window; air quality in NICU
Microbiome protection: favor vaginal delivery if feasible; human milk; minimize broad-spectrum antibiotics; skin-to-skin/KMC promotes maternal microbiome transfer
Growth monitoring: track HC > weight gain early; visceral adiposity risk → avoid excessive weight gain; fat distribution > BMI alone
Discharge counseling: DOHaD ไม่จบที่ NICU — cardiometabolic, neuropsychiatric, renal risk ต้องติดตาม long-term; ไม่ rush formula fortification
Recognize social disparities: 'weathering' + socioeconomic stress + ethnic/cultural differences → growth patterns vary; ideal growth rate ≠ universal — must individualize
Biomarker-informed follow-up: screen for obesity, T2DM, HTN, neurodevelopmental outcomes throughout childhood — FGR/VLBW as high-risk population
14. NICU Practical — DOHaD-Informed Bedside Decisions
ก่อนให้ nutrition plan: ถาม — SGA หรือ VLBW? UA Doppler? แม่มี DM/obesity? Breastfeeding possible? — ตอบแต่ละคำถามเปลี่ยน nutrition strategy
Human milk first: ↓ adiposity, ↓ insulin resistance risk (de Zegher 2013); donor milk ดีกว่า formula ถ้า maternal milk ไม่พอ
Avoid rapid weight gain in SGA: HC gain > weight gain ระยะแรก; catch-up weight ช้าๆ ดีกว่า aggressive ใน first 2 years
Monitor fat distribution ไม่ใช่แค่ weight: visceral > subcutaneous = higher metabolic risk; serial skinfold or abdominal circumference if available
Drug caution: steroids (postnatal), sedatives, analgesics, antibiotics — all during plasticity window → epigenetic effects unknown but real; document indication + duration
Maternal history: GDM, obesity, preeclampsia, infections during pregnancy → flag infant as DOHaD high-risk at discharge → ensure long-term follow-up team aware
ART infants: ↑ imprinting disorder risk; watch for catch-up growth pattern; ↑ BP + altered body composition — needs long-term cardiometabolic follow-up
NICU environment: minimize stress-mediated HPA axis activation (noise, pain, parental separation) — perinatal stress → neuropsychiatric DOHaD risk
15. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Catch-Up Growth Trade-off: อย่า Race to Catch Up ใน SGA
Rapid catch-up growth → ↑ visceral adiposity + skeletal muscle mitochondrial dysfunction + telomere shortening → metabolic syndrome, T2DM, CAD ใน adulthood (Davy P Placenta 2009). Target moderate long-term growth > rapid short-term catch-up. Human milk preferred: SGA + breast milk → ↓ adiposity + normal adiponectin; formula → ↑ fat mass (de Zegher 2013). Head circumference gain > weight gain ระยะแรก = neurocognitive protection first.

PEARL 2 — Mismatch คือ Core Mechanism: Prepare Family for Long-Term Risk
DOHaD ไม่ใช่แค่ 'small baby = sick adult' — ต้องมี mismatch: deprivation in utero + excess after birth. VLBW: deprivation postnatal → catch-up later. LGA: 1st trimester deprivation → 3rd trimester excess → postnatal escalated growth. ทั้งคู่ → adult chronic disease. Family counseling: FGR/VLBW/LGA = DOHaD high-risk group; follow cardiometabolic + neuropsychiatric outcomes throughout childhood. NICU discharge ≠ end of risk.

PEARL 3 — Maternal DM/Obesity → Epigenetic Programming ของลูก
Pima Indian sibship study (Dabelea D Diabetes 2000;49:2208): แม่ที่พัฒนา T2DM → ลูกที่เกิด AFTER มี ↑ T2DM + ↑ BMI กว่าลูกที่เกิด BEFORE — ยืนยัน intrauterine hyperglycemia (ไม่ใช่ genetics) ที่ program ลูก. MEST hypomethylation ใน cord blood จาก GDM mothers (El Hajj 2013). ดังนั้น: เมื่อรับ infant ของแม่ที่มี GDM/obesity → flag as DOHaD high-risk → screen obesity/insulin resistance ตั้งแต่ childhood.

PEARL 4 — Epigenetic Biomarkers: RXRA + PPARG-C1A Predict Adult Adiposity
RXRA methylation ใน umbilical cord → predicts childhood adiposity better than BW หรือ maternal body composition (Godfrey KM Diabetes 2011). PPARG-C1A methylation ใน blood age 5–7y → stable to puberty → predicts adiposity ถึง age 14 (Clarke-Harris 2014). Clinical implication: epigenetic profiling ของ cord blood/early blood sample อาจ identify high-risk infants before phenotype manifests — future of precision neonatology.

PEARL 5 — NICU = Period of Developmental Plasticity: Every Drug and Nutrient Counts
Fanaroff 2025: 'It is always wise to be thoughtful prior to introducing drugs or other interventions that have not been adequately tested, keeping in mind that such alterations during this period of plasticity can also alter the life course.' NICU stressors (toxins, hypoxia, nutrition, infections, inflammation, drugs) → same DOHaD effects as in utero stressors. BPA-free equipment, minimize broad-spectrum antibiotics, promote HM, skin-to-skin/KMC → microbiome + epigenetic protection. เราเป็น physician ที่ set trajectory ชีวิต — ทุก decision มีน้ำหนัก.
16. Evidence Update — Key Studies
Barker DJ et al Ann Hum Biol 2009;36:445: Helsinki Birth Cohort 13,345 persons — link chronic disease + birth weight; placental size/shape → adult HTN/CHD
Calkins K & Devaskar SU Curr Probl Pediatr Adolesc Health Care 2011;41:158: comprehensive fetal origins of adult disease review — DOHaD theoretical framework + mechanisms
Taveras EM et al Pediatrics 2009;123:1177: weight status first 6 months → predicts obesity at age 3 + future mortality — early adiposity trajectory
de Zegher F et al J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2013;98:1242: SGA infants: breast milk → ↓ adiposity + normal adiponectin; formula → ↑ fat mass + ↓ adiponectin
Singhal A et al Am J Clin Nutr 2010;92:1133: 2 RCTs: fortified formula in infancy → ↑ BP + ↑ insulin resistance in childhood — formula DOHaD evidence
Dabelea D et al Diabetes 2000;49:2208: Pima sibship study — intrauterine hyperglycemia (not genetics alone) programs offspring T2DM + obesity
Vaiserman A & Lushchak O Environ Epigenet 2021;7:dvab013: Dutch famine periconceptual exposure → IGF2 hypomethylation 6 decades later → ↑ obesity — foundational epigenetic DOHaD evidence
Godfrey KM et al Diabetes 2011;60:1528: RXRA cord blood methylation → childhood adiposity — epigenetic biomarker stronger than BW
Clarke-Harris R et al Diabetes 2014;63:2528: PPARG-C1A methylation age 5–7y → stable → predicts adiposity to age 14 (EarlyBird 50 study)
Bork-Jensen J et al Diabetologia 2015;58:363: 20 miRNAs ใน skeletal muscle discordant T2DM twins; miR-15b + miR-16 → target INSR/IRS1/PIK3R1
Guénard F et al PNAS 2013;110:11439: maternal bariatric surgery: offspring DNA methylation differences in glucoregulatory + CVD genes before vs after surgery
Al-Haddad BJS et al JAMA Psychiatr 2019;76:594: Swedish birth cohort to age 41: maternal infection → ↑ autism + depression in offspring
Filbin M & Monje M Nat Med 2019;25:367: developmental origins childhood cancer: prenatal genetic hits for leukemias (ETV6-RUNX1, GATA1, MLL) + solid tumors
Manikkam M et al PLoS One 2013;8:e55387: BPA + phthalates → epigenetic transgenerational inheritance of obesity, reproductive disease, sperm epimutations across 3 generations
Davies MJ et al N Engl J Med 2012;366:1803: ART → ↑ prematurity, growth restriction, congenital malformations, imprinting disorders vs spontaneous conception
Davy P et al Placenta 2009;30:539: FGR → accelerated telomere shortening + ↑ cell senescence markers in placenta — mechanism of shortened lifespan with catch-up growth
Eriksson JG et al Eur Heart J 2011;32:2297: Helsinki Birth Cohort: mother's body size + placental size predict CHD in men
Sarkar A et al J Clin Med 2021;10:459: early-life gut microbiome established first 3 years → association with long-term health and disease
17. Self-Check Q&A
Q: Barker hypothesis คืออะไร และ 'thrifty phenotype' หมายถึงอะไร?

ตอบ: Barker hypothesis: LBW ↔ ↑ risk adult CVD, HTN, T2DM, stroke, osteoporosis — established from Dutch famine 1944–45 + replication หลายประชากร. Thrifty phenotype: fetal adaptations ใน adverse environment = energy conservation (sometimes at expense of growth) เพื่อ immediate survival; adaptations remain masked postnatally แต่ tip toward disease เมื่อ stressors เพิ่มขึ้น + environment พลิก.
Q: Mismatch concept คืออะไร และทำไม VLBW infant จึงมีความเสี่ยง DOHaD สูง?

ตอบ: Mismatch: disparity ระหว่าง developmental environment (prenatal/early postnatal deprivation) กับ later abundance → epigenetic phenotype tuned for deprivation exposed to excess → disease. VLBW: deprivation postnatal (NICU period) → catch-up growth post-neonatal = same mismatch. ยิ่ง early deprivation + catch-up → more significant adult consequences.
Q: ทำไม breast milk ดีกว่า formula สำหรับ SGA infants จาก DOHaD perspective?

ตอบ: de Zegher 2013: SGA + breast milk → ↓ adiposity + normal adiponectin; SGA + formula → ↑ fat mass + ↓ adiponectin. Singhal 2010: fortified formula → ↑ BP + ↑ insulin resistance in childhood (2 RCTs). Mechanism: breast milk avoids rapid catch-up weight gain → ↓ visceral adiposity programming → ↓ cardiometabolic adult risk.
Q: อธิบาย epigenetic mechanism ของ DNA methylation: ทำงานอย่างไร และ clinical example คืออะไร?

ตอบ: DNA methylation: CpG island methylation (via Dnmts + SAM) at gene promoters → attracts MeCP2 → histone deacetylation + methylation → condensed heterochromatin → ↓ gene transcription. Example: IGF2 hypomethylation ใน Dutch famine periconceptual survivors → 6 decades later → ↑ obesity (Vaiserman 2021); MEST hypomethylation ใน GDM offspring cord blood → ↑ metabolic risk (El Hajj 2013).
Q: Pima Indian sibship study พิสูจน์อะไรเกี่ยวกับ gestational diabetes?

ตอบ: Dabelea D Diabetes 2000;49:2208: siblings born to same genetically at-risk mother — ลูกที่เกิด AFTER แม่พัฒนา T2DM มี ↑ T2DM risk + ↑ BMI กว่าลูกที่เกิด BEFORE — genetic background identical. ดังนั้น: intrauterine hyperglycemia (ไม่ใช่ genetics เพียงอย่างเดียว) คือ driver ของ metabolic programming ใน offspring.
Q: RXRA และ PPARG-C1A methylation มีนัยสำคัญอย่างไรใน DOHaD?

ตอบ: RXRA: umbilical cord tissue methylation at birth → predicts childhood adiposity — stronger predictor than BW or maternal body composition (Godfrey 2011). PPARG-C1A: blood methylation age 5–7y → stable through puberty → predicts adiposity to age 14 (Clarke-Harris 2014). Significance: epigenetic profiling of cord blood → identify high-risk infants before phenotype manifests → future early intervention.
Q: Maternal overnutrition ส่งผลต่อ neuropsychiatric development ของลูกอย่างไร?

ตอบ: Mechanisms: (1) ↑ proinflammatory cytokines (TLR4 upregulation) → impaired neurogenesis, migration, axon guidance, synapse formation; (2) ↑ leptin/insulin signaling disruption → ↓ cognitive function; (3) HPA axis dysregulation → altered brain signaling; (4) ↓ placental serotonin synthesis → ASD/ADHD/anxiety; (5) ↓ BDNF → cognitive deficits. Outcomes: cognitive impairment, anxiety, depression, schizophrenia, ASD.
Q: Endocrine disruptors ทำให้เกิด transgenerational effects ได้อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Manikkam M 2013: BPA + phthalates → epigenetic changes → pregnant animal (1st gen) exposed → fetus (2nd gen) + gametes of fetus (3rd gen) face same phenotypic consequences — obesity, reproductive disease, sperm epimutations. Mechanism: epigenetic inheritance ผ่าน gametes — methylation pattern ถ่ายทอดข้ามรุ่น. DES classic example: offspring LBW + catch-up growth + adult obesity + abnormal glucose metabolism.
Q: ART infants มีความเสี่ยง DOHaD อย่างไร?

ตอบ: ART (1–3% live births): culture media + techniques → ↑ imprinting disorders (Angelman: 1/400,000 → 1/20,000 = 20x); ↑ prematurity, FGR, congenital malformations (Davies 2012). IVF children: ↓ early growth → catch-up after 6 months → ↑ BP + altered body composition → same DOHaD trajectory as IUGR but from different mechanism (embryo programming, not placental).
Q: NICU nurse บอกว่า baby SGA BW 1.2 kg อายุ 3 สัปดาห์ น้ำหนักขึ้นช้า แม่อยากให้เพิ่ม formula fortification เร็วๆ เพื่อ catch-up เร็ว จะอธิบายอย่างไร?

ตอบ: อธิบาย: catch-up growth เร็วเกินไป ↔ ↑ visceral adiposity + ↑ metabolic syndrome + shortened telomeres (DOHaD evidence: de Zegher 2013; Davy 2009). Target: moderate sustained growth, HC gain > weight gain ระยะแรก. Human milk preferred — ↓ fat mass ใน SGA vs formula. Formula fortification ทำได้ แต่ avoid aggressive escalation เพียงเพื่อ rapid weight catch-up. แจ้ง family ว่า SGA = DOHaD high-risk: screen obesity/insulin resistance ตลอด childhood แม้ BW ดีขึ้น.

Day 113 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.16
บทต่อไป: Chapter 16 — Hypertensive Disorders of Pregnancy. เตรียมตัว: classification (chronic HTN vs GH vs preeclampsia vs eclampsia vs superimposed PE); pathophysiology (spiral artery, angiogenic factors sFlt-1/PlGF, endothelial dysfunction); HELLP syndrome; management antihypertensives ใน pregnancy (labetalol vs nifedipine vs hydralazine); magnesium sulfate: dose, toxicity, antidote; timing of delivery ตาม severity + GA; neonatal implications: prematurity, FGR, thrombocytopenia, neonatal HTN.
